title: carex rostrata subsp. rotundata
intro: Carex rostrata subsp. rotundata, also known as Round-tipped Sedge, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are often attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Carex rostrata subsp. rotundata include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are often used topically to promote wound healing and skin health.
